sources.note: Dong, Mao et al., Machine Learning Bias Correction of HRRR-Smoke Surface PM2.5 Forecasts during the Alaska Wildfire Season, 2026, 3(5), 1263 to 1275, published April 17, 2026. Load-bearing figures: up to 5x surface undercount; CNN1D best corrector, residual about 2x; cause is vertical smoke placement (inversions, fires under cloud).
Second-source confirmation of title, authors, venue, and publication date.
Jingqiu Mao (UAF, NASA Health and Air Quality Science Team) and doctoral student Zhiwei Dong; the UAF smoke/air-quality feed behind Alaska wildfire dashboards.
The operational Alaska wildfire-smoke PM2.5 forecast surface this research improves.
2026 fire season active: 330+ fires tracked, 24,633 lightning strikes in ten days, Interior smoke along the Yukon corridor (early July 2026).
